dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for 78-year-old female with a reported breathing problem.
Hi, I'm Derek, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I've been feeling very short of breath all morning, my legs are swollen.
Can you tell me your name and where you are?
It's Martha, and I'm in my house in Richmond.
What day is today?
April 4th, I think.
Did you know that's a good answer? Thank you.
I'm going to ask you something else. When did the shortness of breath start?
I felt really short of breath, I used my nebulizer but it didn't help much.
Ma'am, we're going to take your blood pressure now.
Her blood pressure is 137 over 68, and she's resting now.
Let me check your breathing-can you take a deep breath for me?
Respirations are 14 per minute, and oxygen saturation is 92% on 2 liters via nasal cannula.
Your airway is clear, and you're speaking normally. Let me check for discoloration in your fingers or toes.
Her pulse is 92 beats per minute and regular.
Your skin is normal color and tone, and I'm seeing some swelling in your lower legs. Would you say they're more swollen than usual?
When did you first notice the shortness of breath and swelling?
Started this morning, around 8-my legs are definitely more swollen than normal.
Any chest pain or coughing as well?
I have some discomfort in my upper right side when I cough. It's not really in my chest.
On a scale of 0 to 10, how would you describe the pain on your right side?
Maybe an 8, it's sharp and getting in the way when I cough.
Have you taken anything else for your breathing?
Just the nebulizer, which I used this morning. It had 2.5 mg albuterol.
What medications do you take at home?
I take Lasix, aspirin, and Vytorin, which contains simvastatin. Oh and I'm on home oxygen-currently on 2 liters.
What is your medical history?
I have high blood pressure and a history of heart failure.
Any known allergies?
I'm allergic to Tylenol.
I'm noticing some old bruises on your abdomen-do they concern you?
They were probably from my shots.
Glucose is normal at 153 mg/dL.
Based on your vitals and EKG, we're following protocol for pulmonary edema related to CHF.
I'll apply a 4-lead EKG-this will help monitor your heart activity.
Now I'll complete a 12-lead EKG for a detailed view of the heart.
Martha, let me check your lungs again to assess for fluid in the bases. Can you breathe with me now?
Clear apical sounds but diminished at the base bilaterally, which is consistent with fluid accumulation.
Let's recheck your breathing and oxygen уровень.
Respirations are now 23 per minute, oxygen saturation is at 95%.
Your blood pressure is still good at 134 over 65. Heart rate is 84 and regular.
Let's reassess the EKG for any changes in rhythm or electrical conduction.
The EKG now shows sinus rhythm with a QRS duration of 110 ms and evidence of left ventricular hypertrophy.
There is also a T wave abnormality in leads I/aVL/V5/V6, which may suggest possible lateral ischemia.
Any change in the pain level on your right side?
It's still around 8 out of 10, still sharp when I cough.
We will transport you to VCU Health Systems Cold for further evaluation.
We'll use the stairchair to move you to the ambulance.
Once we get you to the cot, a team will assist you onto the gurney.
I checked your upper right side again, and could not find any localized tenderness.
We'll check your pressure again now. It's still good at 134 over 65.
Martha, I'll be with you the entire time and continue to monitor your vitals before we transport.
Try to stay relaxed during the move. I've already started an IV with a 18-gauge saline lock in your arm.
Cot is secured on the sidewalk now. She's in a stable position and ready for transport.
Martha, you're doing great. Try to stay relaxed, and I'll keep an eye on your vitals during the ride.
Let me double-check your oxygen and vitals before we move toward transport.
Let me listen to your lungs again-still clear in the upper areas and some dullness in the base.
Your pedal edema is about 2 to 3+ pitting-have you had it this bad in the morning before?
No, usually I'm more swollen at night. This morning is unusual for me.
Blood glucose previously showed 0-it may have been a meter error. Repeating again for confirmation.
EKG findings include possible LVH with anomalies in ST/T waves. We're transmitting this for internal review and transport.
We increased your oxygen to 3 liters and noted the improvement to 97%. We've completed vital checks and EKG.
Vital signs stable with no additional changes to note.
We have an established IV access and an elevated oxygen flow rate. Everything is set for transport.
Rolling her onto the ambulance stretcher now.
Radioing ahead to VCU Health-preparing for transfer with vital information.
EKG still shows sinus rhythm with a 110 ms QRS widening and ST/Т wave changes.
Performed capnography-no bronchospasm detected. Waveform looks normal, no obstruction.
They'll be aware of your medical history, your albuterol use, and that you're allergic to Tylenol.
Martha remains alert and oriented with an E4V5M6 score.
Martha, we're moving you now. I'm right beside you and will keep a close eye on you during transit.
We're starting transport now. I'll update as we move and continue monitoring your vitals.
